2606.25593.title: Low-Complexity Policy Tessellations in Structured Markov Decision Processes
2606.25593.paper_summary: We study optimal-policy geometry in structured Markov decision processes. While approximate dynamic programming and reinforcement learning typically approximate high-dimensional value functions, we show that optimal policies induce simpler decision tessellations. We propose boundary-based policy approximations that learn policy regions directly. A policy-loss decomposition links performance degradation to action margins and explains why errors concentrate near indifference boundaries. Inventory control and queue admission experiments show lower policy error, smaller value gaps, faster error decay, and stability than reinforcement learning baselines.
2606.25593.paper_summary_zh: 我們研究結構化馬可夫決策過程中的最佳政策幾何。雖然近似動態規劃和強化學習通常近似高維價值函數，但我們顯示最佳政策會產生更簡單的決策劃分。 我們提出基於邊界的政策近似，直接學習政策區域。政策損失分解將性能下降與行動邊際聯繫起來，並解釋為什麼錯誤集中在無差異邊界附近。庫存控制和隊列入場實驗顯示出比強化學習基準更低的政策錯誤、更小的價值差距、更快的錯誤衰減和更高的穩定性。
2606.25593.author: Fredy Pokou et.al.
